dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBefore creating an issue\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt's more than likely there is nothing wrong with the library:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIt's very simple; fetch HTML, parse HTML, and search for OpenGraph HTML tags.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUnless HTML or the OpenGraph standard change, the library will not break\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf the target website you are trying to preview redirects you to a login page \u003cstrong\u003ethe preview will fail\u003c/strong\u003e, because it will parse the login page\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf the target website does not have OpenGraph tags \u003cstrong\u003ethe preview will most likely fail\u003c/strong\u003e, there are some fallbacks but in general, it will not work\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eYou cannot preview (fetch) another web page from YOUR web page. This is an intentional security feature of browsers called CORS\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch1 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eDO NOT FETCH CONTENT DIRECTLY FROM A USERS DEVICE. <p dir="auto"><strong>Before creating an issue</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">It's more than likely there is nothing wrong with the library:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>It's very simple; fetch HTML, parse HTML, and search for OpenGraph HTML tags.</li>
<li>Unless HTML or the OpenGraph standard change, the library will not break</li>
<li>If the target website you are trying to preview redirects you to a login page <strong>the preview will fail</strong>, because it will parse the login page</li>
<li>If the target website does not have OpenGraph tags <strong>the preview will most likely fail</strong>, there are some fallbacks but in general, it will not work</li>
<li><strong>You cannot preview (fetch) another web page from YOUR web page. This is an intentional security feature of browsers called CORS</strong></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">DO NOT FETCH CONTENT DIRECTLY FROM A USERS DEVICE. ONLY RUN THIS IN YOUR SERVER AND SANDBOX IT IF YOU CAN</h1><a id="user-content-do-not-fetch-content-directly-from-a-users-device-only-run-this-in-your-server-and-sandbox-it-if-you-can" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: DO NOT FETCH CONTENT DIRECTLY FROM A USERS DEVICE. ONLY RUN THIS IN YOUR SERVER AND SANDBOX IT IF YOU CAN" href="#do-not-fetch-content-directly-from-a-users-device-only-run-this-in-your-server-and-sandbox-it-if-you-can"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
Browsers block this via cors, but you might be clever like a fox and run this in React Native. This is a bad idea, you are exposing the device user to potentially malicious links
<p dir="auto">If you use this library and find it useful please consider <a href="https://github.com/sponsors/ospfranco">sponsoring me</a>, open source takes a lot of time and effort.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Link Preview</h1><a id="user-content-link-preview" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Link Preview" href="#link-preview"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Allows you to extract information from an HTTP URL/link (or parse an HTML string) and retrieve meta information such as title, description, images, videos, etc. via <strong>OpenGraph</strong> tags.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">GOTCHAs</h2><a id="user-content-gotchas" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: GOTCHAs" href="#gotchas"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You cannot request a different domain from your web app (Browsers block cross-origin-requests). If you don't know how <em>same-origin-policy</em> works, <a href="https://dev.to/lydiahallie/cs-visualized-cors-5b8h" rel="nofollow">here is a good intro</a>, therefore <strong>this library works on Node.js and certain mobile run-times (Cordova or React-Native)</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>This library acts as if the user would visit the page, sites might re-direct you to sign-up pages, consent screens, etc.</strong> You can try to change the user-agent header (try with <code>google-bot</code> or with <code>Twitterbot</code>), but you need to work around these issues yourself.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">API</h2><a id="user-content-api" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: API" href="#api"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>getLinkPreview</code>: you have to pass a string, doesn't matter if it is just a URL or a piece of text that contains a URL, the library will take care of parsing it and returning the info o the first valid HTTP(S) URL info it finds.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>getPreviewFromContent</code>: useful for passing a pre-fetched Response object from an existing async/etc. call. Refer to the example below for required object values.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="import { getLinkPreview, getPreviewFromContent } from "link-preview-js";

// pass the link directly
getLinkPreview("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MejbOFk7H6c").then((data) =>
  console.debug(data)
);

////////////////////////// OR //////////////////////////

// pass a chunk of text
getLinkPreview(
  "This is a text supposed to be parsed and the first link displayed https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MejbOFk7H6c"
).then((data) => console.debug(data));

////////////////////////// OR //////////////////////////

// pass a pre-fetched response object
// The passed response object should include, at minimum:
// {
//   data: '<!DOCTYPE...><html>...',     // response content
//   headers: {

//     // should include content-type
//     content-type: "text/html; charset=ISO-8859-1",
//     ...
//   },
//   url: 'https://domain.com/'          // resolved url
// }
yourAjaxCall(url, (response) => {
  getPreviewFromContent(response).then((data) => console.debug(data));
});"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">getPreviewFromContent</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"link-preview-js"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">// pass the link directly</span>
<span class="pl-en">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MejbOFk7H6c"</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span>
  <span class="pl-smi">console</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">debug</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">////////////////////////// OR //////////////////////////</span>

<span class="pl-c">// pass a chunk of text</span>

<span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-smi">console</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">debug</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">////////////////////////// OR //////////////////////////</span>

<span class="pl-c">// pass a pre-fetched response object</span>

<span class="pl-c">// {</span>

<span class="pl-c">//     content-type: "text/html; charset=ISO-8859-1",</span>
<span class="pl-c">//     ...</span>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Options</h2><a id="user-content-options" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Options" href="#options"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Additionally, you can pass an options object which should add more functionality to the parsing of the link</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Property Name</th>
<th align="center">Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>imagesPropertyType (<strong>optional</strong>) (ex: 'og')</td>
<td align="center">Fetches images only with the specified property, <code>meta[property='${imagesPropertyType}:image']</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>headers (<strong>optional</strong>) (ex: { 'user-agent': 'googlebot', 'Accept-Language': 'en-US' })</td>
<td align="center">Add request headers to fetch call</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>timeout (<strong>optional</strong>) (ex: 1000)</td>
<td align="center">Timeout for the request to fail</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>followRedirects (<strong>optional</strong>) (default 'error')</td>
<td align="center">For security reasons, the library does not automatically follow redirects ('error' value), a malicious agent can exploit redirects to steal data, posible values: ('error', 'follow', 'manual')</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>handleRedirects (<strong>optional</strong>) (with followRedirects 'manual')</td>
<td align="center">When followRedirects is set to 'manual' you need to pass a function that validates if the redirectinon is secure, below you can find an example</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>resolveDNSHost (<strong>optional</strong>)</td>
<td align="center">Function that resolves the final address of the detected/parsed URL to prevent SSRF attacks</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>onResponse (<strong>optional</strong>)</td>
<td align="center">Function that handles the response object to allow for managing special cases</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

    "Accept-Language": "fr-CA", // fetches site for French language
    // ...other optional HTTP request headers
  },
  timeout: 1000,
}).then((data) => console.debug(data));"><pre><span class="pl-en">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MejbOFk7H6c"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>

  <span class="pl-c1">headers</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-s">"user-agent"</span>: <span class="pl-s">"googlebot"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c">// fetches with googlebot crawler user agent</span>
    <span class="pl-s">"Accept-Language"</span>: <span class="pl-s">"fr-CA"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c">// fetches site for French language</span>
    <span class="pl-c">// ...other optional HTTP request headers</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">timeout</span>: <span class="pl-c1">1000</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-smi">console</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">debug</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">SSRF Concerns</h2><a id="user-content-ssrf-concerns" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: SSRF Concerns" href="#ssrf-concerns"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Doing requests on behalf of your users or using user-provided URLs is dangerous. One of such attack is trying to fetch a domain that redirects to localhost so the users get the contents of your server (doesn't affect mobile runtimes). To mitigate this attack you can use the resolveDNSHost option:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="// example how to use node's dns resolver
const dns = require("node:dns");
getLinkPreview("http://maliciousLocalHostRedirection.com", {
  resolveDNSHost: async (url: string) => {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const hostname = new URL(url).hostname;
      dns.lookup(hostname, (err, address, family) => {
        if (err) {
          reject(err);
          return;
        }

        resolve(address); // if address resolves to localhost or '127.0.0.1' library will throw an error
      });
    });
  },
}).catch((e) => {
  // will throw a detected redirection to localhost
});"><pre><span class="pl-c">// example how to use node's dns resolver</span>
<span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">dns</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">require</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"node:dns"</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-en">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"http://maliciousLocalHostRedirection.com"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-en">resolveDNSHost</span>: <span class="pl-k">async</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">url</span>: <span class="pl-smi">string</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-v">Promise</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">resolve</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">reject</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-c1">URL</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">url</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
      <span class="pl-s1">dns</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">lookup</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">hostname</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">err</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">address</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">family</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
        <span class="pl-k">if</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">err</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
          <span class="pl-en">reject</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">err</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
          <span class="pl-k">return</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
        <span class="pl-kos">}</span>

        <span class="pl-en">resolve</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">address</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span> <span class="pl-c">// if address resolves to localhost or '127.0.0.1' library will throw an error</span>
      <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">catch</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">e</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c">// will throw a detected redirection to localhost</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This might add some latency to your request but prevents loopback attacks.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Redirections</h2><a id="user-content-redirections" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Redirections" href="#redirections"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Same to SSRF, following redirections is dangerous, the library errors by default when the response tries to redirect the user. There are however some simple redirections that are valid (e.g. HTTP to HTTPS) and you might want to allow them, you can do it via:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="await getLinkPreview(`http://google.com/`, {
  followRedirects: `manual`,
  handleRedirects: (baseURL: string, forwardedURL: string) => {
    const urlObj = new URL(baseURL);
    const forwardedURLObj = new URL(forwardedURL);
    if (
      forwardedURLObj.hostname === urlObj.hostname ||
      forwardedURLObj.hostname === "www." + urlObj.hostname ||
      "www." + forwardedURLObj.hostname === urlObj.hostname
    ) {
      return true;
    } else {
      return false;
    }
  },
});"><pre><span class="pl-k">await</span> <span class="pl-en">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">`http://google.com/`</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">followRedirects</span>: <span class="pl-s">`manual`</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-en">handleRedirects</span>: <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">baseURL</span>: <span class="pl-smi">string</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">forwardedURL</span>: <span class="pl-smi">string</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">urlObj</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-c1">URL</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">baseURL</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">forwardedURLObj</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-c1">URL</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">forwardedURL</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-k">if</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span>
      <span class="pl-s1">forwardedURLObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">===</span> <span class="pl-s1">urlObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">||</span>
      <span class="pl-s1">forwardedURLObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">===</span> <span class="pl-s">"www."</span> <span class="pl-c1">+</span> <span class="pl-s1">urlObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">||</span>
      <span class="pl-s">"www."</span> <span class="pl-c1">+</span> <span class="pl-s1">forwardedURLObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">===</span> <span class="pl-s1">urlObj</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">else</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">onResponse</h2><a id="user-content-onresponse" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: onResponse" href="#onresponse"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">In some cases the website might be missing OpenGraph tags and you might want to provide your own custom logic to try to parse data. For example, if the library is unable to detect a description, you might want to use the text value of the first paragraph instead. This callback gives you access to the Cheerio (the library internally used to parse the HTML) instance, as well as the URL object so you could handle cases on a site-by-site basis, if you need to. This callback must return the modified response object</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="await getLinkPreview(`https://example.com/`, {
  onResponse: (response, doc, URL) => {
    if (URL.hostname == 'example.com') {
        response.siteName = 'Example Website';
    }

    if (!response.description) {
      response.description = doc('p').first().text();
    }
    
    return response;
  },
});"><pre><span class="pl-k">await</span> <span class="pl-en">getLinkPreview</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">`https://example.com/`</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-en">onResponse</span>: <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">doc</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">URL</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-k">if</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">URL</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">hostname</span> <span class="pl-c1">==</span> <span class="pl-s">'example.com'</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
        <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">siteName</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">'Example Website'</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span>

    <span class="pl-k">if</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">!</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">description</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">description</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">doc</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">'p'</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">first</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">text</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span>
    
    <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Response</h2><a id="user-content-response" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Response" href="#response"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Returns a Promise that resolves with an object describing the provided link.
The info object returned varies depending on the content type (MIME type) returned
in the HTTP response (see below for variations of response). Rejects with an error if the response can not be parsed or if there was no URL in the text provided.</p>
